Container.Name: mediahunter
Container.Overview:
Automated missing-content and quality-upgrade search for Sonarr and Radarr.
Features: - Multiple Sonarr/Radarr instances with custom names (HD, 4K, Anime, etc) - Any combination of Sonarr and Radarr, fully optional - SQLite search history and persistent media queue - Per-item cooldown (1–365 days), configurable - Configurable daily search limit (0 = unlimited) - Random jitter on scan intervals (min. 15 min enforced) - Discord webhook notifications (6 events + stats + rate-limit protection) - Periodic statistics report to Discord - Sonarr search granularity: episode / season / series - Upgrade search on/off toggle - Modern 4-theme UI: System, GitHub Inspired, Discord Inspired, Plex Inspired - English-only UI, logs, and Discord messages - React + TypeScript frontend served by Flask - First-run browser wizard (no config editing required) - Optional dashboard/API password protection, CSRF-protected browser writes
Container.Requires: At least one running Sonarr or Radarr instance reachable from the container. Recommended: same Docker network as your Arr stack. Setup connection tests accept only local/private/internal Sonarr/Radarr targets.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the mediahunter web interface.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Stores config.json and the SQLite search history database. Must be a persistent path — without this, the setup wizard restarts on every container restart.

Config.Name: Sonarr API Key
Config.Description: Optional: Sonarr API key for automatic first-run setup. Find it in Sonarr → Settings → General → Security → API Key. If both SONARR_API_KEY and RADARR_API_KEY are set, the wizard is skipped.

Config.Name: Sonarr URL
Config.Description: Optional: Sonarr URL (used only with SONARR_API_KEY). Use the Sonarr container/service IP on the Docker network, e.g. http://172.18.0.10:8989.

Config.Name: Radarr API Key
Config.Description: Optional: Radarr API key for automatic first-run setup. Find it in Radarr → Settings → General → Security → API Key.

Config.Name: Radarr URL
Config.Description: Optional: Radarr URL (used only with RADARR_API_KEY). Use the Radarr container/service IP on the Docker network, e.g. http://172.18.0.11:7878.

Config.Name: Data Directory (env)
Config.Description: Internal container path for the data directory. Change only if you know what you are doing.

Config.Name: Dashboard Password
Config.Description: Optional: if set, the mediahunter dashboard and API require login with this password. Browser write actions are CSRF-protected. Recommended when the WebUI is reachable beyond a trusted LAN.
